steps.title: Convert percentage changes to multipliers
steps.content: When a value increases by 165%, we multiply by $1 + 1.65 = 2.65$. When it decreases by 18%, we multiply by $1 - 0.18 = 0.82$.

steps.title: Calculate the net percentage increase
steps.content: Apply both multipliers in sequence: $$2.65 \times 0.82 = 2.173$$ This means the final value is $2.173$ times the initial value. Converting to a percentage increase: $$(2.173 - 1) \times 100\% = 117.30\%$$ That confirms A is correct.
